_meta.description: Company context ontology for resume feedback scoring. Used to calibrate credibility weights, domain boosts, and contextual signals when evaluating candidate experience.

schema.tier: TIER_0 (quant/elite) > TIER_1 (FAANG+) > TIER_2 (strong brand) > TIER_3 (solid mid) > TIER_4 (early/unknown)
schema.baseCredibility: 0.0–1.0 scalar applied to all role claims from this employer
schema.domainBoost: Per-domain multiplier on top of baseCredibility; 'default' is fallback
schema.recentSignals: Observable signals from Q4-2025 / Q1-2026 affecting interpretation
schema.scoringNotes: Human-readable guidance for resume reviewer logic

feedback.note: Pinnacle quant firm. Any role here is an exceptional signal — bar for both technical and intellectual ability is extraordinarily high.
Top-tier quant hedge fund and market maker. Engineering and research roles carry enormous weight. Citadel Securities is separate but equally prestigious.
Legendary quant fund — arguably the most selective employer in the world. Hiring is near-invisible but carrying this name is an elite signal.
Elite quant / tech hybrid. Highly selective across both finance and engineering tracks.
Top HFT/market-making firm. Engineering bar rivals quant funds. Strong signal for systems-level and backend roles.
Quant fund with strong engineering culture. ML and data science roles here are particularly prestigious.
FAANG core. Extremely strong signal across nearly all engineering disciplines. Google DeepMind / Brain experience is elite-tier for AI/ML roles.
FAANG core with one of the strongest AI research labs (FAIR). High comp, intense culture. Reality Labs (VR) experience carries less weight than core infra/AI.
FAANG core. Azure and AI/Copilot experience are especially strong signals. GitHub and LinkedIn are strong subsidiaries. Known for better WLB than Meta/Amazon.
FAANG core. Extremely strong for hardware, silicon, and mobile. Secretive culture — experience here signals high security clearance on impactful products.
FAANG — AWS experience is particularly strong. Core Amazon e-commerce carries good but slightly lower signal than AWS/infrastructure. Known for intense culture and high attrition.
AI-native Tier-1. Extremely selective and at the frontier of AI safety and capability research. Hiring aggressively in 2026 while legacy tech sheds headcount. Any role here is elite signal.
AI-native Tier-1. ChatGPT pioneer. Undergoing significant internal pressure from Anthropic and Google competition, but still an elite brand. Headcount doubling per 2026 reports.
First company to hit $5T valuation — the defining infrastructure company of the AI era. Experience here is a very strong signal across GPU, systems, and AI infrastructure.
High engineering bar — highly regarded for backend, payments, and developer tooling. One of the largest active hirers in 2026 (506 open roles per tracker data).
Known for freedom-and-responsibility culture and top-of-market compensation. High performance bar. Engineering work at Netflix is highly regarded.
Category leader in data + AI. Largest active open-role footprint in 2026 (841 roles). Strong signal for data engineering, MLOps, and Lakehouse-era skills.
Network/security category leader. Strong signal for distributed systems and edge computing. Actively hiring in 2026.
Dominant observability platform. Strong signal for DevOps, SRE, and cloud infrastructure roles.
Cloud data warehouse leader. Strong signal for data engineering and analytics engineering roles.
Dominant CRM and enterprise cloud brand. Good signal for enterprise software roles. Layoffs in 2025 but remains a respected employer.
Cult-like culture with strong signal for data platform and defense-adjacent roles. High Blind ratings. Experience here polarizes interviewers — context matters.
Strong engineering signal especially for distributed systems, ML platform, and mapping. Good brand recognition globally.
Strong engineering culture, well-regarded for product engineering and data science. Well-known consumer brand.
Microsoft subsidiary. Good signal, especially for ML and data engineering. Benefits from Microsoft parentage on resume.
Developer-first communications platform. Strong signal for API/platform and backend roles.
Category leader for e-commerce infrastructure. Strong product engineering culture. Remote-first company.
Strong employer brand with high Blind ratings. Known for employee wellbeing. Good signal for creative tools, ML (Firefly), and enterprise software.
Dev tooling leader (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket). Remote-first culture. Cut 10% workforce in 2026 but hired 800 AI-focused replacements.
Fastest growing GPU cloud company. Key AI infrastructure player. Hiring aggressively in 2026. Very strong signal for infra and cloud engineering.
Global payments infrastructure leader — the Stripe of enterprise. Strong signal for payments and backend engineering. Actively hiring in 2026.
Leading crypto exchange. Strong signal for Web3 and crypto-adjacent roles. Some volatility tied to crypto market cycles.
Consumer fintech with strong mobile and backend engineering. Brand is well-known but reputation is mixed outside engineering circles.
Legacy enterprise vendor undergoing aggressive AI transformation. Major layoffs in 2026 (30k). Experience here is solid but carries less prestige than hyperscalers.
Legacy hardware/enterprise brand. Shedding traditional roles in 2026 to fund AI infrastructure. Signal is decent for hardware and enterprise infra.
Legacy chip maker in aggressive turnaround mode. Deep technical signal for chip design and compiler work. $1B cost reduction target in 2026, significant layoffs.
Legacy tech giant. Solid signal for enterprise and consulting roles. Red Hat subsidiary carries stronger signal than IBM brand alone.
Enterprise HR/Finance SaaS leader. Laid off 8.5% of workforce in 2025 to reallocate to AI. Good signal for enterprise software roles.
Leading ITSM/enterprise workflow platform. Growing AI feature set. Good signal for enterprise software engineering.
Consumer social brand. Good signal for mobile, AR, and ML. Company has gone through multiple rounds of layoffs which slightly dampens the signal.
Strong engineering culture, especially for ML recommendations and data engineering. Good brand recognition globally.
Post-acquisition volatility has significantly changed the signal. Pre-2022 Twitter experience carries stronger weight than post-Musk. xAI is a separate entity with higher signal.
MBB prestige — top management consulting firm. Strong signal for strategy, PM, and consulting roles. Less signal for pure engineering unless data/analytics division.
MBB prestige. Similar to McKinsey — strong for strategy and PM, moderate for technical roles.
MBB prestige. Slightly smaller footprint than McKinsey/BCG but equivalent prestige within consulting.
Large consulting/services firm. Signal varies greatly by division and project. Less prestigious than MBB but a known brand.
Big Four consulting/audit. Moderate signal — strong for audit/finance, weaker for pure engineering.
FinTech company (Square + Cash App). Significant 4,000-person layoffs in early 2026, primarily customer support cut by AI. Technical roles less affected.
Financial data infrastructure — often called the 'plumbing of fintech'. Strong signal for backend and API-focused engineering.
Category-defining design tool. Strong signal for frontend, product design, and developer tooling roles.
Company not found in ontology. Apply conservative baseline credibility. Request reviewer to look up company size, funding, and reputation independently.

recentSignalGlossary.aggressiveHiring: Growing headcount significantly amid market-wide freeze.
recentSignalGlossary.hypergrowth: Headcount or revenue growing >50% YoY; may be high risk / high reward.
recentSignalGlossary.activelyHiring: Company has significant open-role footprint and is not in freeze mode.
recentSignalGlossary.stableGrowth: Steady, moderate growth — no major layoffs or red flags.
recentSignalGlossary.stableHeadcount: No major layoffs or expansions; holding pattern.
recentSignalGlossary.highCompensation: Known for top-of-market total compensation packages.
recentSignalGlossary.highGrowth: Meaningful revenue or valuation growth in the past 12 months.
recentSignalGlossary.massiveLayoffs30k_2026: Oracle laid off ~30,000 employees in early 2026.
recentSignalGlossary.layoffs2026RealityLabs: Meta laid off ~1,500 from Reality Labs in early 2026.
recentSignalGlossary.aiPivot: Company is cutting legacy roles to reinvest in AI infrastructure.
recentSignalGlossary.aiRolePivot: Company cut general headcount but opened AI-specific replacement roles.
recentSignalGlossary.aiRealignment: Budget reallocated toward AI; some roles cut, new AI roles added.
recentSignalGlossary.aiExpansion: Actively building AI features or products; relevant for AI/ML candidates.
recentSignalGlossary.aggressiveAIInvestment: Committing very large capex to AI (models, infra, chips).
recentSignalGlossary.aggressiveAIProductRollout: Shipping AI products rapidly to market (Copilot, etc.).
recentSignalGlossary.AIBoomBeneficiary: Company's core product directly benefits from the AI infrastructure buildout.
recentSignalGlossary.aiCustomerServiceReplacement: Headcount cut because AI agents are replacing customer service functions.
recentSignalGlossary.aiFeatureExpansion: Actively adding AI features to existing product surface.
recentSignalGlossary.claudeCodeSuccess: Anthropic's Claude Code has achieved strong enterprise adoption in 2026.
recentSignalGlossary.enterpriseMomentum: Growing enterprise customer base and revenue.
recentSignalGlossary.regulatoryFocus: Company under increasing regulatory scrutiny or proactively engaging regulators.
recentSignalGlossary.codeRedPressure: OpenAI's leadership declared code-red on product competitiveness.
recentSignalGlossary.doublingHeadcount: OpenAI reportedly planning to double headcount in 2026.
recentSignalGlossary.anthropicCompetitionRising: Anthropic gaining significant market share vs OpenAI.
recentSignalGlossary.geminiCompetition: Google Gemini competing intensely with Claude and GPT.
recentSignalGlossary.turnaroundEffort: Company in multi-year turnaround (e.g., Intel under new CEO).
recentSignalGlossary.unstableReputation: Employer brand is volatile or polarizing among candidates.
recentSignalGlossary.xAISeparate: xAI is Elon Musk's separate AI company, distinct from X Corp.
recentSignalGlossary.highlyRatedCulture: Consistently high employee satisfaction ratings (e.g., Glassdoor, Blind).
recentSignalGlossary.unverified: Company not in ontology — score is conservative baseline only.
recentSignalGlossary.steadyGrowth: Company growing at a healthy, sustainable pace.
recentSignalGlossary.appleIntelligenceRollout: Apple Intelligence (AI features) being rolled out across devices.
recentSignalGlossary.stableRestructuring: Ongoing slow restructuring but no dramatic events.

TIER_0.label: Quant / Pinnacle Elite
TIER_0.guidance: Unconditionally strong signal. Rigorous intellectual and technical bar. Apply maximum credibility multiplier.

TIER_1.label: FAANG+ / AI-Native Tier-1
TIER_1.guidance: Strong brand, high engineering bar. Apply high credibility multiplier. Note domain-specific boosts carefully.

TIER_2.label: Strong Brand / Scale-up / Category Leader
TIER_2.guidance: Solid signal. Company is well-known, product is proven. Engineering bar is high but not FAANG-level.

TIER_3.label: Solid Mid-Tier / Legacy Enterprise / Consultancy
TIER_3.guidance: Moderate signal. Brand is recognizable. Weight domain-specific experience carefully. Watch for layoff signals.

TIER_4.label: Early Stage / Unknown / Unverified
TIER_4.guidance: Apply conservative baseline. Request reviewer to verify independently. May be great (early-stage startup) or weak.
